summary: Frontend-only testing of expandable normalization sections in Folder Comparison view. All 9 core functionality tests pass. Both Spontaneous Activity and Light HRA expandable sections work correctly with proper collapse/expand behavior, arrow animations, and color schemes.

issues: Light HRA Folder Average Baseline BF shows 101.2% instead of 100% - This is by design as Light data is normalized against Spontaneous baseline BF (77.9) rather than Light baseline (78.8). Agent context confirms this is intentional for cross-section comparison.

critical_code_review_comments: Both expandable sections use data-testid attributes correctly (expand-spont-norm, expand-light-norm)
Arrow rotation animation (rotate-90 class) works correctly for expand/collapse toggle
Spontaneous Activity normalized section: Folder Average baseline columns correctly show 100.0%
Light HRA normalized section: Uses cohort spontaneous baseline for normalization (intentional per agent context)
Color scheme consistent: amber/orange for baseline, purple for drug, cyan for light columns
Percentage (%) headers correctly displayed in normalized tables
seed_data_creation: Used existing Test Experiments folder with 2 recordings (4D030006, 4D030007)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Expandable normalization sections fully tested. Spontaneous Activity section shows baseline=100% correctly. Light HRA section normalizes against spontaneous baseline (not its own baseline), showing 101.2% - this is intentional per agent context. All expand/collapse animations work. Color schemes verified: amber for baseline, purple for drug, cyan for light.

features_verified.spont_section_collapsed_default: ✅ Collapsed by default with arrow pointing right
features_verified.spont_color_scheme: ✅ Amber for baseline, purple for drug

notes: Light HRA section baseline shows 101.2% because it normalizes against spontaneous cohort baseline BF (77.9 bpm), not the Light section's own baseline (78.8 bpm). Per agent context: 'Both use the same cohort baseline average from the spontaneous activity section for normalization.' This allows cross-section comparison. Calculation: 78.8/77.9*100 = 101.2%
